req.title: Manager creates benefit
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a benefit through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers benefit creation via REST API with mandatory field validation and audit logging. I designed five tests: (1) EP positive – valid manager payload returns 200/201 with entity id and audit record; (2) NEG – missing mandatory field returns 400; (3) NEG – unauthenticated request returns 401; (4) DT – forbidden role (employee) returns 403; (5) BVA – benefit name at maximum allowed length (255 chars) is accepted. Together these cover the happy path, all three error pathways, and a boundary condition on a string field, satisfying the minimum positive/negative/boundary requirement with classical heuristic coverage.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_benefit_successfully
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
All mandatory fields are provided with valid values
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token>
POST /api/benefits with a complete, valid benefit payload
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
An audit record exists for the created benefit via <get_audit_record>
trace: R-WEB-033: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_manager_creates_benefit_successfully(base_url, get_manager_token, get_audit_record, unique_benefit_name):
    token = get_manager_token()
    payload = {
        "name": unique_benefit_name(),
        "type": "health",
        "description": "Standard health coverage",
        "effective_date": "2024-01-01"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/benefits", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code} {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id") or body.get("benefit_id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"

    audit = get_audit_record(entity_type="benefit", entity_id=entity_id)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist for the created benefit"
    assert audit.get("action") == "create"

tests.name: test_create_benefit_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Mandatory field 'name' is omitted from the payload
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token>
POST /api/benefits with a payload that omits the mandatory 'name' field
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing the missing field
trace: R-WEB-033: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_benefit_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(base_url, get_manager_token):
    token = get_manager_token()
    payload = {
        "type": "health",
        "description": "Missing name field",
        "effective_date": "2024-01-01"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/benefits", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400 but got {resp.status_code} {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "name" in error_text, "Validation error must reference the missing 'name' field"

tests.name: test_create_benefit_unauthenticated_returns_401
actions: POST /api/benefits with a valid payload but no Authorization header

tests.name: test_create_benefit_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated as an employee (non-manager role)
All mandatory fields are provided with valid values
actions: Obtain a valid employee auth token via <get_employee_token>
POST /api/benefits with a complete, valid benefit payload

tests.name: test_create_benefit_name_at_max_length_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Benefit name is exactly 255 characters (assumed maximum allowed length)
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token>
POST /api/benefits with a benefit name of exactly 255 characters
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
The persisted benefit name matches the 255-character input
trace: R-WEB-033: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-033: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 255

def test_create_benefit_name_at_max_length_accepted(base_url, get_manager_token):
    token = get_manager_token()
    max_name = "B" * MAX_NAME_LENGTH
    assert len(max_name) == MAX_NAME_LENGTH

    payload = {
        "name": max_name,
        "type": "dental",
        "description": "BVA max-length name test",
        "effective_date": "2024-06-01"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/benefits", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 but got {resp.status_code} {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id") or body.get("benefit_id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"
    assert body.get("name") == max_name, "Persisted name must match the 255-character input"
